message:
Expand main.rs with additional functionality

Major expansion of main application logic with enhanced features
and capabilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
-{"id":"bd-152","title":"Implement session diff logic","description":"Deliverables:
- DiffResult struct:
  - new_domains: Vec<String>
  - removed_domains: Vec<String>
  - changed_domains: Vec<(String, i64, i64)> (domain, old_count, new_count)
  - new_processes: Vec<String>
  - provider_changes: HashMap<Provider, (i64, i64)>

- compute_session_diff(old_run_id: &str, new_run_id: &str, threshold_pct: f64) -> DiffResult:
  - Query domain counts for each session
  - Compute set differences
  - Filter changes by threshold

- CLI: rano diff --old <id> --new <id> [--threshold <pct>] [--json]
- Pretty output: sections for each diff type
- JSON output: DiffResult as JSON

- Unit tests for diff computation
- E2E test with seeded sessions","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:13.251408999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:20:36.202144009Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-152","depends_on_id":"bd-1o5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:13.269634692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- DiffResult struct:

  - changed_domains: Vec<(String, i64, i64)> (domain, old_count, new_count)
  - new_processes: Vec<String>
  - provider_changes: HashMap<Provider, (i64, i64)>

- compute_session_diff(old_run_id: &str, new_run_id: &str, threshold_pct: f64) -> DiffResult:
  - Query domain counts for each session
  - Compute set differences
  - Filter changes by threshold

- CLI: rano diff --old <id> --new <id> [--threshold <pct>] [--json]
- Pretty output: sections for each diff type
- JSON output: DiffResult as JSON

- Unit tests for diff computation
- E2E test with seeded sessions","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:13.251408999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:11:33.071836807Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T03:11:33.071351032Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-152","depends_on_id":"bd-1o5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:13.269634692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15l","title":"Add ancestry CLI flag and SQLite storage","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI flag: --show-ancestry (default: false)
- Config key: show_ancestry=true

- Event output with ancestry:
  Pretty: '2026-01-20T10:00:00Z | + connect | anthropic | zsh → tmux → claude (pid=1234) | ...'
  JSON: add 'ancestry': ['zsh(100)', 'tmux(200)', 'claude(1234)']

- SQLite schema update:
  - Add 'ancestry_path' TEXT column to events table
  - Store as comma-separated: 'zsh:100,tmux:200,claude:1234'
  - Index on ancestry_path for queries

- Report enhancement:
  - Group by top-level parent (first in chain)
  - Show: 'Spawned from zsh: 45 connections, tmux: 23 connections'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:20.237572582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:53.478986533Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:53.478919266Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15l","depends_on_id":"bd-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:20.308039948Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15l","depends_on_id":"bd-vb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:17.270912144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-15l","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented --show-ancestry flag + config key (show_ancestry). Event output now includes ancestry chain (pretty) and JSON ancestry array. Added ancestry_path column/index to events and write path as comm:pid chain. Report now includes Spawned From section (top-level parent counts) and JSON ancestry_roots. Note: cargo test -q attempted but was blocked by concurrent cargo lock in shared env.","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gl","title":"Implement export subcommand","description":"Deliverables:
- Add 'export' to Command enum
- ExportArgs struct with all filter fields
- parse_export_args() function

- run_export() function:
  1. Open SQLite (error if missing)
  2. Build query with WHERE clause from filters
  3. Stream rows to stdout in requested format
  4. Handle Ctrl+C gracefully (don't corrupt output)

- format_csv_row(row) function:
  - Escape quotes by doubling
  - Wrap fields with commas/quotes/newlines in quotes
  - Join with comma, output CRLF

- format_jsonl_row(row) function:
  - Serialize row to JSON object
  - Output with LF

- Memory efficiency:
  - Use SQLite row iteration, not collect()
  - Flush stdout periodically

- Error handling:
  - Missing SQLite file: clear error message
  - Empty result set: output header only (CSV) or nothing (JSONL)
  - Invalid filter: exit with error before query","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:42.648931351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:34.850375758Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:34.850143029Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gl","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:42.684768291Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gl","depends_on_id":"bd-2a6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:57.715021911Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-1gl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented export subcommand in src/main.rs (CLI parsing, query builder, CSV/JSONL formatting, streaming + flush, Ctrl+C handling). Added unit tests for csv_escape/glob_to_sql_like/validate_fields. cargo test -q passed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1i6","title":"Connection retry detection","description":"Purpose:
- Detect and highlight reconnection patterns (same remote, repeated connect/close cycles)
- Surface rate limiting, network issues, or bugs in AI clients

User impact:
- See warning: '⚠ Retry pattern: 5 connections to api.anthropic.com:443 in 30s'
- Identify problematic connection behavior without manual analysis
- Correlate retries with provider responses

Features:
- Track connection events per (remote_ip, remote_port, pid)
- Configurable threshold: --retry-threshold <N> (default 3)
- Configurable window: --retry-window-ms <N> (default 60000)
- Output retry warnings inline with events
- Store retry_count in SQLite for analysis

Implementation:
- HashMap<(IpAddr, u16, u32), Vec<Instant>> for recent connections
- On close event: check if count in window exceeds threshold
- Prune old entries on check (sliding window)

Testing:
- Unit tests for retry detection logic
- E2E script that simulates rapid reconnects
- Verify warnings appear and SQLite has retry counts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:56.071844384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:30:25.978010206Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:30:25.977937749Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i6","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:56.101171213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jj","title":"Design alert system architecture","description":"Deliverables:
- AlertConfig struct with all threshold fields
- Decision on pattern matching library (regex vs glob vs both)
- Define alert event structure for SQLite storage
- Define alert output format (stderr, prefix, colors)
- Decide on alert state tracking (cooldown to avoid spam)

Design questions to answer:
1. Should alerts be per-connection or per-threshold-breach?
2. How to handle alert cooldown (same alert within N seconds)?
3. Should we track 'alert acknowledged' state?
4. How do alerts interact with --json output mode?

Output:
- Design doc in docs/alert-design.md
- Updated schema proposal for events table","design":"Design decisions (pre-answered to speed implementation):
1. Alerts should be per-connection (each matching event triggers alert, subject to cooldown)
2. Cooldown: HashMap<AlertType, Instant> - skip alert if same type within cooldown_ms
3. No 'acknowledged' state for v1 - keep it simple, user handles alerts
4. JSON mode: alerts as JSON objects on stderr with alert:true field, same structure as events

Pattern matching approach:
- Use glob for simple wildcards (most common case)
- Allow regex with r: prefix for power users (e.g., 'r:.*evil.*')
- Default to glob if no prefix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:28.505557159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:38:54.195422658Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:38:54.195219435Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jj","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:28.527150866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ju","title":"Process ancestry chain display","description":"Purpose:
- Show full process tree path for spawned AI processes
- Enable understanding of multi-layer orchestration (shell → tmux → agent → claude)
- Critical for debugging and auditing agent swarms

User impact:
- 'rano --show-ancestry' displays: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → bash(789) → claude(1234)'
- Users understand exactly how AI processes were spawned
- Helps identify rogue or unexpected process spawning

Features:
- --show-ancestry flag: include ancestry in event output
- Ancestry format: 'parent_comm(pid) → ... → current_comm(pid)'
- Cache ancestry to avoid repeated /proc walks
- Store ancestry_path in SQLite for historical queries
- Report includes ancestry breakdown

Implementation approach:
- read_ancestry(pid) walks /proc/{pid}/stat PPID chain to init
- Cache: HashMap<pid, Vec<(pid, String)>> with TTL
- Ancestry invalidated if PID recycled (check comm matches)

Testing:
- Unit tests for ancestry chain parsing
- E2E script with nested process tree (shell → script → rano)
- Verify ancestry displayed correctly in output and stored in SQLite","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:18.765913116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:31.933812417Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:31.933767403Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete: ancestry chain reading (bd-vb7), CLI flag + SQLite storage (bd-15l), and tests + docs (bd-94z). Feature fully implemented with 21 unit tests and E2E coverage.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ju","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:18.871921353Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1lq","title":"Colorblind-friendly themes","description":"Purpose:
- Make rano accessible to users with color vision deficiency
- ~8% of males have some form of color blindness

User impact:
- '--theme colorblind' uses patterns and symbols in addition to colors
- High-contrast palette visible to all users
- Equal usability regardless of color perception

Features:
- New theme: colorblind
- Use unicode markers: ● anthropic, ◆ openai, ▲ google, ○ unknown
- High-contrast palette: blue/orange/black (deuteranopia-friendly)
- Patterns in bar charts: █▓▒░ for differentiation
- Shapes before provider names in stats

Implementation:
- Add theme variants to existing Theme enum
- Map provider to (color, symbol) tuple
- Update format_* functions to include symbol when theme=colorblind

Testing:
- Unit tests for symbol mapping
- E2E script verifying symbols appear in output
- Visual comparison screenshots (docs)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:15.507701965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:15.536926441Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lq","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:15.536896755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1o5","title":"Session diff between runs","description":"Purpose:
- Compare what changed between monitoring sessions
- Enable baseline comparison for security audits
- Detect new domains, removed endpoints, or changed patterns

User impact:
- 'rano diff --old <session1> --new <session2>' shows changes
- Auditors can baseline 'normal' and detect deviations
- Regression detection for AI client updates

Features:
- rano diff --old <run-id|session-name> --new <run-id|session-name>
- Output sections:
  - New domains: domains in new but not old
  - Removed domains: domains in old but not new
  - Changed counts: domains with >50% count change
  - New processes: process names that appeared
  - Provider shifts: significant count changes per provider
- --format pretty|json
- --threshold <percent> for 'significant' change (default 50)

Implementation:
- Query both sessions from SQLite
- Build sets/maps per domain, process, provider
- Compute diff and format

Testing:
- E2E script that creates two sessions with known differences
- Verify diff output shows expected changes
- Test empty diff (identical sessions)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:25.791936234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:25.822159100Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o5","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:25.822132831Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Testing:

 {"id":"bd-1vz","title":"Export to common formats (CSV/JSONL)","description":"Purpose:
- Enable users to bring rano data into external tools (Excel, Pandas, grep, jq)
- Unlock SQLite data for analysis pipelines
- Replace need for manual sqlite3 queries

User impact:
- 'rano export --format csv > connections.csv' produces Excel-compatible output
- 'rano export --format jsonl | jq .domain' enables shell pipelines
- Data analysts can use familiar tools instead of learning SQLite views

Features:
- rano export --format csv|jsonl [filters]
- Filters: --since, --until, --run-id, --provider, --domain
- Output: streaming to stdout (pipeable)
- Fields: ts, run_id, event, provider, pid, comm, cmdline, proto, local, remote, domain, duration_ms
- CSV: header row, proper quoting, RFC 4180 compliant
- JSONL: one JSON object per line (reuses existing JSON formatting)

Constraints:
- Must handle large datasets without loading all into memory
- Must not require external dependencies (no csv crate)
- Field order must be stable (documented)

Testing:
- Unit tests for CSV quoting edge cases (commas, quotes, newlines)
- E2E script that exports, validates format, re-imports to verify roundtrip
- E2E must test empty result set, large result set, special characters","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:23.163975299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:58:33.633478143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:58:33.633432227Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1vz","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:23.193760593Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-20u","title":"Implement alert threshold checking","description":"Deliverables:
- AlertConfig struct with fields:
  - domain_patterns: Vec<String>
  - max_connections: Option<u64>
  - max_per_provider: Option<u64>
  - duration_threshold_ms: Option<u64>
  - alert_unknown_domain: bool
  - bell_on_alert: bool
  - cooldown_ms: u64

- check_alert() function called on each event
- AlertState struct tracking:
  - Last alert time per threshold type
  - Cooldown enforcement
  - Total alert count

- Integration points:
  - Call check_alert() after event detection
  - Return AlertResult { triggered: bool, alert_type: AlertType, message: String }

Implementation notes:
- Pattern matching: use glob crate for wildcards, regex for advanced
- Cooldown: HashMap<AlertType, Instant> for last trigger time
- Thread-safe if alerts need cross-thread state","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:37.468718314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:21:16.751560546Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:21:16.751513828Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20u","depends_on_id":"bd-1jj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:09.769903852Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20u","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:37.481921179Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-23q","title":"Preset tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_load_builtin_preset() - audit preset loads correctly
  - test_load_user_preset() - custom preset from disk
  - test_preset_not_found() - error for unknown preset
  - test_preset_override_by_cli() - CLI flags win over preset
  - test_multiple_presets_merge() - later preset overrides earlier
  - test_list_presets_includes_builtin() - all builtins listed

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/presets.sh):
  - Test each built-in preset runs without error
  - Create custom preset, verify it loads
  - Verify CLI flag overrides preset value
  - Test --list-presets output format
  - Log: preset content, effective config, actual behavior

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Presets'
  - Table of built-in presets with descriptions
  - Example: creating custom preset
  - Troubleshooting: preset not found","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:58.076841992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:15:36.249033974Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:15:36.248981866Z","close_reason":"Added 16 unit tests for presets. Created E2E test (scripts/e2e/presets.sh) with 11 tests. Added Presets section to README with built-in presets table, custom preset examples, merging docs, and troubleshooting.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23q","depends_on_id":"bd-26d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:58.096911567Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-23q","depends_on_id":"bd-3t7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:03.125194958Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -14,9 +14,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-2a6","title":"Design export format and CLI","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI design document:
  rano export [OPTIONS]
  --format csv|jsonl (required)
  --sqlite <path> (default: observer.sqlite)
  --since <timestamp|relative> (e.g., 24h, 7d, 2026-01-20)
  --until <timestamp|relative>
  --run-id <id>
  --provider <name> (repeatable)
  --domain <pattern> (repeatable)
  --fields <list> (optional: override default field list)
  --no-header (CSV only: skip header row)

- Define field order and names:
  ts, run_id, event, provider, pid, comm, cmdline, proto, local_ip, local_port, remote_ip, remote_port, domain, duration_ms

- CSV format spec:
  - RFC 4180 compliant
  - Fields with commas/quotes/newlines properly escaped
  - UTF-8 encoding
  - CRLF line endings (for Excel compatibility)

- JSONL format spec:
  - One JSON object per line
  - UTF-8 encoding
  - LF line endings
  - Same fields as CSV

- Output: docs/export-design.md","design":"Implementation notes:
- CSV escaping: double quotes to escape quotes, wrap fields containing comma/quote/newline
- No external CSV crate needed - simple escaping logic
- Streaming: use SQLite row iteration with step(), don't collect()
- Memory: flush stdout every 1000 rows
- Empty result: CSV outputs header only, JSONL outputs nothing
- Progress: no progress bar (would interfere with piping)
- Errors: fail fast on invalid filter, print to stderr

Query template:
SELECT ts, run_id, event, provider, pid, comm, cmdline, proto,
       local_ip, local_port, remote_ip, remote_port, domain, duration_ms
FROM events
WHERE 1=1
  [AND ts >= :since]
  [AND ts < :until]
  [AND run_id = :run_id]
  [AND provider IN (:providers)]
  [AND domain LIKE :domain_pattern]
ORDER BY ts ASC","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.336206689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:44:57.416472667Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:44:57.416429906Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2a6","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.364391899Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fm","title":"Alert tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_alert_domain_pattern_match() - glob and regex patterns
  - test_alert_max_connections_threshold() - trigger at exact threshold
  - test_alert_cooldown_prevents_spam() - no re-alert within cooldown
  - test_alert_duration_threshold() - long-running connections flagged
  - test_alert_unknown_domain() - unresolved domains trigger alert
  - test_alert_config_validation() - invalid patterns rejected

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/alert-thresholds.sh):
  - Use mock process that makes N connections
  - Verify domain watch alert triggers on matching domain
  - Verify max-connections alert at threshold
  - Verify cooldown prevents duplicate alerts
  - Verify alerts appear in stderr, not stdout
  - Verify SQLite has alert=true for alert events
  - Log: inputs, expected alerts, actual alerts

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Alert Thresholds'
  - Example use cases: security audit, rate limit detection

 {"id":"bd-2iu","title":"Alert thresholds and watch mode","description":"Purpose:
- Transform passive monitoring into active alerting
- Allow users to set guard rails and not watch output constantly
- Enable security-focused workflows with domain watching

User impact:
- 'rano --alert-domain evil.com --alert-max-connections 100' instantly flags suspicious activity
- Auditors can set thresholds and walk away
- Alerts are prominent and logged separately

Features:
- --alert-domain <pattern>: Regex/glob for domains to highlight/warn
- --alert-max-connections <N>: Warn when total connections exceed N
- --alert-max-per-provider <N>: Warn when any provider exceeds N
- --alert-duration-ms <N>: Flag connections lasting longer than N ms
- --alert-unknown-domain: Warn on any connection to unresolved domain

Output behavior:
- Alerts print to stderr with [ALERT] prefix
- Alerts use distinct color (red/yellow)
- Optional --alert-bell to ring terminal bell
- Alert events logged to SQLite with alert=true flag

Testing:
- Unit tests for threshold checking and pattern matching
- E2E script that triggers each alert type and validates output
- E2E script must verify alerts don't fire for normal traffic

Constraints:
- Must not add latency to main polling loop
- Patterns must be case-insensitive and support wildcards","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:19.697313595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:37:55.697417500Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:37:55.697362296Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2iu","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:19.713686680Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-300","title":"Add config subcommand CLI","description":"Deliverables:
- Add 'config' to Command enum with subcommands:
  - check: validate all config files
  - show: display resolved config
  - paths: show config file locations

- ConfigSubcommand enum:
  - Check
  - Show { json: bool }
  - Paths

- run_config_check():
  1. Find all config files (config.conf, rano.toml, presets)
  2. Run validation on each
  3. Print errors/warnings with file:line context
  4. Exit with appropriate code

- run_config_show(json: bool):
  1. Load all config as if starting monitor
  2. Print resolved key=value (or JSON)
  3. Show source of each value (default, config, cli)

- run_config_paths():
  1. List search paths for each config type
  2. Mark which files exist
  3. Show load order

- Output formatting:
  - Errors: red with ✗ prefix
  - Warnings: yellow with ⚠ prefix
  - Info: normal with → prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:30.990453145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:29.974960495Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:29.974891415Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-300","depends_on_id":"bd-52z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:44.515420210Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-300","depends_on_id":"bd-n2d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:31.003915277Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-36b","title":"Shell prompt integration","description":"Purpose:
- Provide one-line status for embedding in shell prompts (PS1, starship)
- Always-visible network status without dedicating a terminal

User impact:
- Prompt shows: '🔵 3 active | anthropic:45 openai:12'
- Instant awareness of AI network activity
- Configurable format for different prompt systems

Features:
- rano status --one-line: output for prompt embedding
- rano status --format <template>: custom format string
- Fast execution (<50ms) for prompt responsiveness
- Read from SQLite or shared state file

Template variables:
- {active}: current active connection count
- {total}: total connections this session
- {anthropic}, {openai}, {google}: per-provider counts
- {last_domain}: most recent domain
- {session_name}: current session name

Default format: '{active} active | anthropic:{anthropic} openai:{openai}'

Implementation:
- Query latest session from SQLite
- Cache results for 1s to avoid repeated queries
- Optional daemon mode for even faster response

Testing:
- Unit tests for template expansion
- E2E script verifying output format
- Benchmark: must complete in <50ms","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:36.403153804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:36.420956080Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36b","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:36.420930913Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dy","title":"Implement retry detection logic","description":"Deliverables:
- RetryTracker struct:
  - recent: HashMap<(IpAddr, u16, u32), Vec<Instant>>
  - threshold: usize (default 3)
  - window_ms: u64 (default 60000)

- track_connection(key: &ConnKey, pid: u32) -> Option<RetryWarning>:
  - Add current timestamp to Vec for (ip, port, pid)
  - Prune entries older than window_ms
  - If count >= threshold, return warning

- RetryWarning struct:
  - count: usize
  - window_seconds: f64
  - endpoint: (IpAddr, u16)

- Integration: call after close event detection","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:09:44.229045587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:42:52.524153483Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:42:52.524109651Z","close_reason":"Implemented RetryTracker struct with track_connection method as specified. RetryWarning struct with count, window_seconds, and endpoint fields. Includes prune_stale method for memory management. Tests pass, code compiles. Integration into main loop and CLI flags are in dependent bead bd-90z.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dy","depends_on_id":"bd-1i6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:09:44.287016248Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3o1","title":"rano v2: alerting, export, UX improvements","description":"Goals:
- Add proactive alerting capabilities for security monitoring
- Enable data export for integration with external tools
- Improve UX with presets, ancestry display, and accessibility
- Reduce friction through config validation and session naming

Context:
- Current rano is observation-only; users must watch output constantly
- SQLite data is locked inside; no easy export to CSV/Pandas/grep
- Config debugging requires trial-and-error runs
- Session IDs are opaque (PID-timestamp) not human-readable

Scope:
- 5 prioritized features with comprehensive tests and documentation
- Each feature is independent but builds on existing infrastructure
- Preserve all existing CLI behavior; improvements are additive

Quality bar:
- Every feature must include unit tests and at least one e2e test script
- E2E scripts must use the existing harness (scripts/e2e/lib.sh)
- Tests must run offline without elevated privileges
- Documentation updates for README and --help text

Acceptance:
- Each child feature has tasks with unit tests + e2e scripts + docs
- All new flags documented in README
- Config validation catches common errors before runtime","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:06.726854553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:06.726854553Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-lr2","title":"Alert output formatting and logging","description":"Deliverables:
- Alert output to stderr with distinct formatting:
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:00Z | domain-watch | api.unknown.com matched '*unknown*'
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:01Z | max-connections | 101 connections (threshold: 100)
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:02Z | long-duration | connection to api.anthropic.com:443 lasted 45000ms

- Colors:
  - Alert prefix: bright red background
  - Alert type: yellow
  - Details: white

- Bell: if --alert-bell, output \a after alert line

- JSON mode: alerts as JSON objects on stderr with 'alert': true field

- SQLite: add 'alert' boolean column to events table
  - Alerts logged with alert=true for later querying

- Summary: include alert count in final summary
  'Alerts triggered: 5 (3 domain-watch, 2 max-connections)'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.170178182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:35.010307656Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:35.010262661Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-20u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.488350819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-291","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.788894643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.200230439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-n2d","title":"Config validation command","description":"Purpose:
- Catch configuration errors before runtime
- Reduce debugging time for 'why isn't my config working'
- Provide clear feedback on config file issues

User impact:
- 'rano config check' validates all config files and reports issues
- 'rano config show' displays resolved configuration
- Clear error messages point to exact file:line of problems

Features:
- rano config check: validate config.conf, rano.toml, presets
- rano config show: display resolved config as key=value
- rano config show --json: display as JSON for automation
- rano config paths: show where config files are loaded from

Validation checks:
- Syntax: valid key=value, valid TOML
- Known keys: warn on unknown keys (typo detection)
- Value types: numbers parse, booleans valid
- Patterns: regex/glob patterns compile
- Paths: referenced files exist (log_dir, sqlite)
- Provider config: patterns are valid, mode is merge/replace

Exit codes:
- 0: valid configuration
- 1: errors found (won't run)
- 2: warnings only (will run but may have issues)

Testing:
- Unit tests for each validation check
- E2E script with invalid configs to verify error messages","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.564851300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:27:38.305576490Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:27:38.305506198Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n2d","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.594253010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-uj6","title":"Export tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_csv_escape_quotes() - 'foo"bar' -> '"foo""bar"'
  - test_csv_escape_comma() - 'foo,bar' wrapped in quotes
  - test_csv_escape_newline() - embedded newlines wrapped
  - test_csv_header_generation() - correct field order
  - test_jsonl_valid_json() - each line parses as JSON
  - test_export_empty_result() - no crash on empty data

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/export-formats.sh):
  - Seed SQLite with test data
  - Export to CSV, validate header and row count
  - Export to JSONL, validate each line is valid JSON
  - Test --since/--until filters
  - Test --provider filter
  - Test --domain filter
  - Roundtrip: export CSV, import to new SQLite, compare counts
  - Log: filter inputs, row counts, sample output

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Exporting Data'
  - Example: 'rano export --format csv --since 24h > daily.csv'
  - Example: 'rano export --format jsonl | jq -r .domain | sort | uniq -c'
  - Field reference table","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:52.683237477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:58:11.050454256Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:58:11.050411055Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uj6","depends_on_id":"bd-1gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:58.073466211Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-uj6","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:52.694725221Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vb7","title":"Implement ancestry chain reading","description":"Deliverables:
- read_ancestry(pid) -> Vec<(u32, String)>:
  - Walk /proc/{pid}/stat to get PPID
  - Recursively read parent until PPID=0 or PPID=1
  - Return chain from root to current: [(init_pid, init_comm), ..., (pid, comm)]

- AncestryCache struct:
  - cache: HashMap<u32, (Vec<(u32, String)>, Instant)>
  - ttl: Duration (default 30s)
  - get_or_compute(pid) method with staleness check

- Staleness detection:
  - If cached entry exists, verify current comm matches cached comm
  - If mismatch (PID recycled), invalidate and recompute

- format_ancestry(chain) -> String:
  - Output: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → claude(789)'
  - Truncate if >5 levels: '... → parent(456) → claude(789)'
  - Handle single-level: 'claude(789)'

- Integration:
  - Add ancestry_cache to main state
  - Lookup ancestry when --show-ancestry enabled
  - Pass ancestry to event formatting","design":"Implementation approach:
- Reuse existing read_ppid() from descendant tracking
- Stack-based iteration (not recursion) to avoid stack overflow
- Stop at PPID=0 (kernel) or PPID=1 (init/systemd)
- Reverse to get root→leaf order

Performance considerations:
- Cache is essential - ancestry changes rarely
- 30s TTL is reasonable (processes don't typically reparent)
- Staleness check: comm is sufficient (PID reuse with same comm is rare)
- Memory: ~100 bytes per cached entry, max ~1000 tracked PIDs = 100KB

Edge cases:
- Process exits during walk: return partial chain with '?' for missing
- Zombie process: use comm from cmdline fallback
- Init containers: stop at container init, not host init","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.224824805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:11:08.313186516Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:11:08.313142694Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.294596381Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-vb7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented ancestry chain reading + cache in src/main.rs (read_ancestry, AncestryCache w/ TTL + PID reuse check, format_ancestry truncation). Integrated optional ancestry output into connect/close event formatting (pretty + JSON) gated by args.show_ancestry. Added show_ancestry field in MonitorArgs. cargo test -q passed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:10:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","title":"rano vNext: accuracy + insight + performance","description":"Goals:
- Make domain attribution accurate and trustworthy when possible.
- Make SQLite logging obviously useful through reporting and live insights.
- Improve performance and reliability under high event rates.
- Keep UX simple: defaults work, advanced options are discoverable.

Context:
- Current capture is PTR-only and can misattribute domains.
- SQLite has strong schema/views but no first-class reporting UX.
- Event rates can spike and cause sqlite overhead.
- Provider mapping is fixed to substring matching; users need overrides.

Scope:
- Implement 5 prioritized improvements with tasks, tests, and docs.
- Preserve existing CLI behavior; improvements are additive.

Quality bar:
- Every feature must include comprehensive unit tests and at least one e2e test script.
- E2E scripts must include detailed, human-readable logging (inputs, expected outputs, actual outputs).
- Tests must run offline without elevated privileges unless explicitly marked otherwise.

Acceptance:
- Each child epic has tasks with unit tests + e2e scripts + docs.
- All new features degrade gracefully (no sudo/libpcap, missing config).

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.5.5","title":"E2E: live stats views rotation","description":"Deliverables:
- E2E script that runs stats views (provider/domain/port/process) and captures output.
- Use shared e2e harness/log format.
- Log file must include: view sequence, sample outputs, and any rendering warnings.

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.6","title":"Secondary ideas backlog (documented)","description":"Purpose:
- Preserve the remaining 25 ideas in a self-contained bead so future work doesn’t need the original plan.
- These are valuable but lower priority vs the top 5.

Ideas:
1) Resolver cache with TTL for PTR.
2) Focus mode for new connections in a time window.
3) Session replay summary from SQLite.
4) UDP deduping window to reduce noise.
5) Ignore localhost/private IP toggles.
6) Provider confidence indicator.
7) Process tree display at startup.
8) Minimal quiet mode.
9) Full-screen TUI.
10) CSV/JSON export of stats.
11) Include/Exclude CIDR filter flags.
12) Permission diagnostics for /proc issues.
13) SQLite rotation / retention controls.
14) `--pid` direct tracking.
15) macOS/Windows support via native APIs.
16) Auto-detect terminal width for bars.
17) SNI mapping decay policy controls.
18) DoH resolver option.
19) Replace manual /proc parsing with `procfs` crate.
20) Summarized exit report (top N, totals).
21) Live "last 5 minutes" window view.
22) Multi-panel paging + hotkeys.
23) Per-provider allow/deny lists.
24) Log JSON schema versioning.
25) Health check subcommand.

Guidance:
- Each idea can be promoted to its own feature/task bead when prioritized.
- Consider grouping by UX, performance, portability.

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.7","title":"Test harness + logging standard for E2E","description":"Purpose:
- Ensure every e2e script has consistent, high-signal logging and reproducible behavior.

Deliverables:
- Shared e2e runner (shell or Rust) that standardizes log format:
  - header with timestamp, host, OS, rano version, args
  - inputs/fixtures summary
  - expected outputs
  - actual outputs (truncated with pointers)
  - pass/fail summary
- Standard log location convention (e.g., `logs/e2e/<test-name>-<ts>.log`).
- Helper functions for asserting output (string/regex) with detailed diffs.

Notes:
- Keep runner minimal; do not require elevated privileges.
- All e2e beads must depend on this harness.
","notes":"Added E2E harness: scripts/e2e/run.sh + scripts/e2e/lib.sh with standard log header, log dir logs/e2e/<name>-<ts>.log, outputs captured in logs/e2e/outputs. Helpers: e2e_run + assert_status/contains/regex/eq with truncated output and pointers. Added scripts/e2e/README.md.
